subprocess.continuation_prompt:
{% if attempt %}Bug fix is still in progress (attempt #{{attempt}}).
Resume from the current workspace state. Your previous investigation context is preserved.
Do not re-investigate issues you have already identified.
Focus on completing the fix and verification.{% endif %}

prompt_template:
You are fixing a bug reported in NullTickets task `{{task.identifier}}`

{% if attempt %}Continuation context:
- This is retry attempt #{{attempt}}.
- Resume from the current workspace state instead of restarting from scratch.
- Your previous investigation results are preserved in the workspace.
{% endif %}

Bug report:
Identifier: {{task.identifier}}
Title: {{task.title}}

Description:
{% if task.description %}{{task.description}}{% else %}No description provided.{% endif %}

Bug fix workflow:

1. This is an unattended orchestration session. Never ask a human to perform follow-up actions.
2. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth/permissions/secrets).

Phase 1 - Reproduce:
- Read the bug report carefully. Identify the expected vs actual behavior.
- Find the relevant code paths.
- Write a failing test that reproduces the bug. Run it to confirm it fails.
- If you cannot reproduce, document what you tried and why it did not reproduce.

Phase 2 - Root cause:
- Trace the execution path that leads to the bug.
- Identify the root cause, not just the symptom.
- Document your findings before making changes.

Phase 3 - Fix:
- Make the minimal change that fixes the root cause.
- Avoid unrelated refactoring or cleanup.
- Ensure the failing test now passes.
- Run the full test suite to check for regressions.

Phase 4 - Verify:
- Confirm the fix addresses the original bug report.
- Verify no new test failures were introduced.
- Commit with a clear message referencing the bug.
- Push to a feature branch and create a pull request.

Quality bar:
- The fix is minimal and targeted.
- A regression test exists for the bug.
- No unrelated changes.
- All tests pass.
